description: Bharat Kavach Phase 1 evaluation dataset v3. Transcripts: 100+ scam (India-specific + Jim Browning real calls + English scam scripts) + 100+ legit (India-specific + English non-scam scripts). Currency: 176 genuine + 25 counterfeit (19 real + 6 augmented). Documents: 25 authentic + 25 forged.

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as courier company representative): 'Ma'am, we have a parcel booked under your name and Aadhaar number heading to Taiwan. It has been intercepted and contains banned narcotics. This is a serious matter. I am transferring your call to the cyber crime cell right now, please stay on the line.' [Call transferred] Caller 2 (posing as police officer): 'Your Aadhaar has been used for money laundering linked to a narcotics case. You are now under investigation. Do not disconnect this call or inform anyone, or you will be arrested immediately.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: 'Elderly woman falls prey to digital arrest, loses Rs 3.09 crore' — Deccan Herald, https://www.deccanherald.com/amp/story/india%2Fkarnataka%2Felderly-woman-falls-prey-to-digital-arrest-loses-rs-3-09-cr-3674822

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as TRAI officer): 'Sir, your mobile number has been used to send threatening messages to several people including public figures. This number will be blocked in two hours unless you cooperate. I am connecting you to Maharashtra Police for verification.' Caller 2 (posing as police sub-inspector): 'Several FIRs have been registered against you. You need to verify your bank accounts with us right now to prove your innocence, otherwise a warrant will be issued for your arrest today.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: 'Delhi Police Busts Digital Arrest Scam, Recovers Rs 2 Crore' — All India Radio News, https://www.newsonair.gov.in/delhi-police-busts-digital-arrest-scam-recovers-%e2%82%b92-crore-duped-from-92-year-old

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as post office official): 'A parcel sent in your name to China has come back. It contains 150 grams of MDMA. This carries a minimum prison term of over 70 years under narcotics law.' Victim: 'I never sent any parcel.' Caller: 'It was sent using your ID card details. We can help you get a no-objection certificate, but you will need to pay a processing amount immediately, and you must not tell your family until this is resolved.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: Deccan Herald, https://www.deccanherald.com/amp/story/india%2Fkarnataka%2Felderly-woman-falls-prey-to-digital-arrest-loses-rs-3-09-cr-3674822

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as bank compliance officer): 'Sir, a new SIM card has been fraudulently activated in your name and used for illegal calls. We need to transfer you to the concerned police station for verification.' Caller 2 (posing as sub-inspector): 'Please provide your and your spouse's bank account details so we can verify and freeze any suspicious activity. You will be refunded after verification. Do not discuss this with anyone as it is part of an ongoing investigation.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: 'Karnataka woman loses Rs 3.16 crore in digital arrest scam' — Deccan Herald, https://www.deccanherald.com/amp/story/india%2Fkarnataka%2Fkarnataka-woman-loses-rs-316-crore-in-digital-arrest-scam-3617765

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as CBI officer over video call): 'Your Aadhaar card was used to open a bank account involved in a money laundering case connected to a national security matter. The Supreme Court has issued a warrant against you. If you disconnect this call, a team will be sent to arrest you immediately. You must remain visible on this video call at all times until we clear this matter.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: 'The Growing Problem of Digital Arrest Scams in Bharat' — VIF, https://www.vifindia.org/article/2024/november/26/The-Growing-Problem-of-Digital-Arrest-Scams-in-Bharat

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as customs official): 'A parcel addressed to you was seized at Mumbai airport containing narcotics and foreign currency. This is a customs violation and a criminal case will be registered against you today unless you pay a customs clearance penalty within the hour via bank transfer.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: VIF report on Noida IT engineer case, https://www.vifindia.org/article/2024/november/26/The-Growing-Problem-of-Digital-Arrest-Scams-in-Bharat

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as investigating officer over sustained video call): 'You are under digital arrest. You are not permitted to leave the frame or contact anyone outside this call until the investigation into your involvement in this financial fraud case is complete. Any attempt to disconnect will be treated as evidence of guilt.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: 'India's digital arrest scams' — Lowy Institute, https://www.lowyinstitute.org/the-interpreter/india-s-digital-arrest-scams

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as bank fraud department): 'We have detected suspicious activity on your account linking it to a case under investigation by the Enforcement Directorate. To avoid your account being frozen and a formal case being filed, you need to move your funds to a safe verification account temporarily, and keep this confidential as it is a sensitive ongoing matter.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: VIF general modus operandi, https://www.vifindia.org/article/2024/november/26/The-Growing-Problem-of-Digital-Arrest-Scams-in-Bharat

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as RBI compliance officer): 'Your bank account has been flagged in a nationwide money laundering probe. We are legally required to place you under digital surveillance during this call. Please do not inform your family as this is a confidential national security matter, and follow the fund transfer instructions we provide to clear your name.'
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: VIF / National Cybercrime Reporting Portal data, https://www.vifindia.org/article/2024/november/26/The-Growing-Problem-of-Digital-Arrest-Scams-in-Bharat

samples.transcript: Caller (posing as Supreme Court officer with forged order): 'We are sending you a Supreme Court order via WhatsApp right now. This is case number SC 4421/2024. You must remain on this video call, lock your room, and do not open the door for anyone. We will conduct a digital search operation via video shortly. Disconnecting will be treated as contempt of court.',
samples.source_citation: Adapted from: SC suo motu concern over forged court orders — AIR News, https://www.newsonair.gov.in/sc-takes-strong-note-of-rising-digital-arrest-scams-seeks-centre-and-cbi-response

samples.transcript: Caller (lawyer, to client): 'I reviewed the FIR copy you sent me. The case has been listed for hearing next month at the district court. I will email you the summons details and we can discuss your response over the weekend.'
